data.title: Anthropic: There is insufficient legal basis to designate it as a national security supply chain risk.
data.description: On March 5, Anthropic CEO Dario Amodei issued a statement saying that Anthropic received a formal letter from the U.S. Department of Defense (now known as the War Department) on March 4, confirming that the company had been identified as a national security supply chain risk. Amodei stated that this designation lacks sufficient legal basis and the company will challenge it through legal channels. Amodei pointed out that the wording of the Department of Defense letter indicates that the designation only applies to situations where customers directly use Claude in Department of Defense contracts and does not affect Claude usage scenarios unrelated to Department of Defense contracts. He reiterated that Anthropic's negotiations with the Department of Defense have been ongoing, and both sides have communicated on service methods and transition plans. Anthropic maintains two bottom lines: opposition to large-scale domestic surveillance and fully autonomous weapons. Amodei apologized for a previously leaked internal company post, stating that the post was written on the same day that Trump announced the removal of Anthropic, Secretary of Defense Hegseth issued the supply chain risk designation, and OpenAI reached an agreement with the Pentagon, and that the tone was not fully considered. He stated that Anthropic's top priority is ensuring that frontline warfighters and national security experts do not lose critical tools during major operations, and the company will continue to provide model services to the Department of Defense and national security agencies at a nominal fee.

data.title: A wallet associated with the Pump.fun team deposited 1.757 billion Pumps, worth $3.54 million, into Bitget.
data.description: According to Onchain Lens monitoring, a wallet associated with the Pump.fun team deposited 1.757 billion PUMPs, worth $3.54 million, into Bitget. The wallet currently holds 12.3 billion PUMPs, worth $24.77 million.

data.title: Anthropic Opposes Autonomous Weapons and Domestic Surveillance
data.description: Anthropic has reiterated its stance against fully autonomous weapons and extensive domestic surveillance. According to Jin10, the company plans to offer its models to the U.S. Department of Defense and the national security sector at a symbolic cost. Additionally, Anthropic's engineers will provide ongoing technical support to these entities. This move underscores the company's commitment to ethical considerations in the deployment of AI technologies.

data.title: Shenzhen Stock Exchange Updates Hong Kong Stock Connect Securities List
data.description: The Shenzhen Stock Exchange has announced updates to the Hong Kong Stock Connect securities list. According to Jin10, Muyuan Foods and Han's CNC have been added to the list of eligible securities. These changes will take effect on March 6. The inclusion of these companies is part of ongoing efforts to enhance the connectivity between the mainland and Hong Kong financial markets, providing investors with more diverse investment opportunities.

data.title: On Polymarket, the probability of OKX's IPO in 2026 is currently estimated at 17%.
data.description: Data from Polymarket shows that the probability of OKX's IPO in 2026 has fallen from a high of 57% in early January to a current 17%, with trading volume exceeding $400,000. Previously, it was reported that ICE, the parent company of the New York Stock Exchange, invested in OKX at a valuation of $25 billion. ICE declined to disclose the specific investment amount or terms, but emphasized the shared vision between the two companies.

data.title: London Faces Decline in Child Population Due to Housing and Childcare Costs
data.description: London is experiencing a significant decline in its child population, more so than any other region in Britain, according to a report by the London Assembly. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting that the primary factors contributing to this trend are the high costs of housing and childcare, which are prompting young families to relocate. The report underscores the urgent need for policy interventions to address these affordability issues and retain families in the capital. The findings suggest that without action, the demographic shift could have long-term implications for the city's economy and community structure.

data.title: Palantir Secures Court Order Against Former Employees Over AI Startup
data.description: Palantir Technologies has obtained a court order preventing three former employees from recruiting its staff and utilizing confidential information for their artificial intelligence startup. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting the legal victory for Palantir as it seeks to protect its intellectual property and workforce. The court's decision underscores the importance of safeguarding proprietary information in the competitive tech industry. This development comes amid increasing concerns over data security and employee mobility in the rapidly evolving AI sector.

data.title: Asia's Foreign-Exchange Reserves Bolster Currency Defense Amid Middle East Conflict
data.description: Asia's central banks are leveraging their substantial foreign-exchange reserves, totaling $8 trillion, to fortify their currencies as the Middle East conflict intensifies, leading to rising energy prices. Bloomberg posted on X that this development poses a significant risk to the region's oil-importing economies. The reserves provide these economies with a buffer to manage currency fluctuations and mitigate the impact of increased energy costs. As the situation in the Middle East continues to evolve, Asian countries are closely monitoring the potential economic repercussions and adjusting their strategies accordingly.

data.title: An Ethereum ICO wallet, dormant for 10.6 years, has been activated. An initial investment of $125 is now worth $834,000, representing a return of approximately 6,687 times.
data.description: According to Onchain Lens monitoring, an Ethereum ICO wallet that had been dormant for 10.6 years was activated, transferring 100.27 ETH, worth approximately $212,000, to a new wallet. This address initially purchased 401.1 ETH for only $125, and its current total holdings are worth approximately $834,000, representing an estimated return of about 6,687 times.

data.title: Iranian Revolutionary Guard Spokesperson Warns of Intensified Attacks in Coming Days
data.description: The spokesperson for Iran's Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps has announced that attacks are expected to become more intense in the coming days. According to Jin10, this statement comes amid escalating tensions in the region. The spokesperson did not provide specific details about the nature or targets of these attacks, but the warning has heightened concerns about potential conflicts. Observers are closely monitoring the situation as it unfolds, with implications for regional stability and international relations.

data.title: Venezuela and U.S. Agree to Restore Diplomatic Relations
data.description: Venezuela has confirmed its agreement with the United States to restore diplomatic and consular relations. According to Jin10, this development marks a significant step in improving bilateral ties between the two nations. The agreement comes after years of strained relations, with both countries expressing a willingness to engage in dialogue and cooperation. This move is expected to facilitate better communication and collaboration on various issues of mutual interest. The restoration of diplomatic relations is seen as a positive development in international diplomacy, potentially leading to enhanced economic and political interactions between Venezuela and the United States.

data.title: Solv Protocol: Vault suffers limited vulnerability attack, affecting 38 SolvBTC.
data.description: Solv Protocol announced on the X platform that a limited exploit occurred in its BRO vault, affecting fewer than 10 users and impacting 38.0474 SolvBTC. All other vaults and user funds remain safe and unaffected. The team is investigating with security partners and has taken steps to prevent similar incidents from recurring, while also covering any losses incurred by affected users. Solv Protocol has offered a 10% white-hat bounty to the attacker, demanding the timely return of the funds. Hypernative Labs, SlowMist, and CertiK provided timely alerts regarding this incident.

data.title: The UAE is considering freezing billions of dollars in Iranian assets.
data.description: According to a Wall Street Journal report citing sources familiar with the matter, Iran has launched more than 1,000 drones and missiles at targets within the United Arab Emirates (UAE). In retaliation for this action, the UAE is considering freezing billions of dollars worth of Iranian assets held in its possession, a move that could sever one of Iran's most vital economic lifelines. If the UAE takes action, Iran's access to foreign exchange and its participation in global trade networks will be severely restricted, and its domestic economy, already struggling with inflation, will be significantly hampered. UAE officials have privately warned Iran of potential action. According to informed officials, potential measures include freezing the assets of UAE-owned "shadow companies" used to conceal trade activities, and a comprehensive financial crackdown on local currency exchange institutions used to circumvent formal banking channels for transferring funds. In addition to financial measures, policymakers are also considering direct maritime action, such as seizing Iranian vessels. (Jinshi)

data.title: JGB Futures Rise as Stock Market Faces Challenges
data.description: Japanese Government Bond (JGB) futures experienced a slight increase, reflecting a cautious sentiment in the stock market. Wall Street Journal (Markets) posted on X, highlighting the ongoing volatility in equity markets, which has prompted investors to seek safer assets like government bonds. The rise in JGB futures indicates a shift in investor preference amid uncertainties in the global economic landscape. Analysts suggest that the stock market's weakness is driven by concerns over economic growth and geopolitical tensions, leading to increased demand for stable investments. As the market navigates these challenges, JGB futures are likely to remain a focal point for investors looking to mitigate risk.

data.title: Japan's Stock Market Experiences Early Decline
data.description: Japan's stock market saw a decrease in early trading, with the Nikkei Average reported at 55,026 points, down by 251 points, marking a 0.45% decline. According to RTHK, this drop reflects the initial market sentiment. The Nikkei's performance is closely watched as it provides insights into the broader economic trends in Japan.

data.title: Japanese and South Korean stock markets opened lower, with South Korea's KOSPI index narrowing its losses to less than 1%, after falling as much as 2% in early trading.
data.description: South Korea's KOSPI index opened down 89.35 points, or 1.6%, at 5494.55 on Friday, March 6. Japan's Nikkei 225 index opened down 481.74 points, or 0.87%, at 54796.32 on Friday, March 6. (Jinshi)

data.title: US Defense Secretary says military action against Iran will escalate further.
data.description: On March 5, local time, U.S. Defense Secretary Hergsays stated that U.S. military operations against Iran would escalate further, with a "significant increase" in airstrikes over Tehran, the Iranian capital. Hergsays said the U.S. military would deploy more fighter squadrons, increase combat and defensive capabilities, and raise bomber sortie rates, but did not disclose specific military details. He stated that airstrikes against Iran and its capital Tehran would "increase significantly" in the near future, and that the U.S. military was receiving more support from overseas bases. (Jinshi)

data.title: Californian Billionaires Navigate Fortune Amid US-Israel Strikes on Iran
data.description: In the past week, the intersection of technology and warfare has brought both fortune and challenges to three Californian billionaires. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting the impact of the US and Israel's military actions against Iran on these individuals. As geopolitical tensions rise, their ventures in technology have seen significant shifts, reflecting the broader implications of international conflicts on business and innovation.

data.title: Iran's Retaliation Threat Impacts Strategic Waterway Traffic
data.description: Iran's potential retaliation against Israeli and American military actions has led to restricted movement through a key strategic waterway. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting the geopolitical tensions affecting global trade routes. This development is part of a broader context of escalating regional conflicts, which could have significant implications for international shipping and energy markets.

data.title: U.S. Military to Intensify Operations Against Iran, Says Defense Secretary
data.description: U.S. Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth announced on March 5 that military actions against Iran are set to escalate significantly. According to Jin10, Hegseth stated that the U.S. military will increase firepower over Tehran, Iran's capital, with plans to deploy more fighter squadrons and enhance operational and defensive capabilities. The frequency of bomber missions will also rise, although specific military details were not disclosed. Hegseth emphasized that the intensity of strikes against Iran and Tehran will notably increase in the coming period, with the U.S. military receiving additional support from overseas bases.
